rulings.Case Title: JOANNE O. DENHAM et al VS. DOUGLASS INSULATION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 23, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. No opposition filed. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNE O. DENHAM et al VS. DOUGLASS INSULATION CO., INC. et al
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 23, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 1, Plaintiffs¿ motion for summary adjudication as to defendant The Scotts Co. is off calendar at the moving party¿s request. = (503/TLJ)

rulings.Case Title: DONALD SILVER et al VS. CROWN CORK & SEAL COMPANY, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss Or Stay For Forum Non Conveniens
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR THURSDAY, FEBRUARY 23, 2012 IN DEPARTMENT 503 AT 9:30 A.M., LINE 2, Defendant American Honda Motor Co.¿s motion to dismiss the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is denied. Defendant failed to demonstrate that Oregon is a suitable alternative forum because it did not show that all remaining defendants are subject to jurisdiction there. (See Stangvik v. Shiley, Inc. (1991) 54 Cal.3d 744; American Cemwood Corp. v. American Home Assurance Co. (2001) 87 Cal.App.4th 431.) Defendant¿s alternate motion to stay the action on the grounds of forum non conveniens is granted. Defendant successfully demonstrated that California is a seriously inconvenient forum as the balance of private and public interest factors makes it just that the litigation proceed in Oregon. The action is stayed until such time as plaintiff has obtained jurisdiction over all defendants in Oregon and, at that time, the case will be dismissed. If, after filing in Oregon, plaintiff can conclusively show to this court that certain defendants are not subject to personal jurisdiction in Oregon, the court will entertain a request to lift the stay. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 10:30am, not 9:30am. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If a court reporter is needed, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/TLJ)

The action is stayed until such time as plaintiff has obtained jurisdiction over all defendants in Oregon and, at that time, the case will be dismissed.
